title: grass_fire
description: A grass fire ignites along a highway median and spreads rapidly across open fields toward farmland.

timeline.title: Fire Ignition Reported
timeline.description: Motorists report flames in a highway median. Assess size and direction of spread.

timeline.title: Fast Flame Spread
timeline.description: Grass fire moving at high speed across dry fields. Alert agricultural stakeholders.

timeline.title: Highway Closure Needed
timeline.description: Smoke reducing visibility on the highway. Coordinate road closure with traffic control.

timeline.title: Farm Structures Threatened
timeline.description: Fire approaching a farmstead with fuel storage tanks. Defensive action required.

timeline.title: Backfire Considered
timeline.description: Crews evaluating use of a prescribed backfire to deprive the main fire of fuel.

timeline.title: Fire Controlled
timeline.description: Backfire successful. Main fire meets burned area and stops. Begin overhaul.

node-1.title: Grass Fire Along Highway
node-1.situation: A grass fire has ignited in the dry median of a rural highway. Winds are 25 mph and the grass is bone dry after several weeks without rain. The fire is spreading fast.
node-1.question: What is your first priority?

options.label: A
options.text: Request fire crews, initiate highway closure for smoke visibility, and warn nearby farms.

options.label: B
options.text: Send a single tanker truck and monitor before escalating.

options.label: C
options.text: Focus entirely on traffic control and leave fire suppression to state resources.

node-2.title: Farmstead Threatened
node-2.situation: The fire has spread across two fields and is approaching a farmstead with propane tanks and a grain silo. The farmer is still on the property.
node-2.question: How do you handle the civilian on the property?

options.label: A
options.text: Order immediate evacuation of the farmer and family, then position a tanker to protect the tanks.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow the farmer to assist in defending the property to save resources.

node-3.title: Backfire Decision
node-3.situation: The fire is 300 meters away and closing fast. Crews recommend igniting a backfire from a dirt road to consume the fuel ahead of the main fire. Conditions are suitable.
node-3.question: Do you authorize the backfire?

options.label: A
options.text: Authorize the backfire with proper lookouts and escape routes established.

options.label: B
options.text: Decline the backfire and commit crews to direct attack on the fire front.

node-4.title: Backfire in Progress
node-4.situation: The backfire is burning toward the main fire. Wind shifts slightly, and crews need to adjust their position to remain safe.
node-4.question: How do you manage crew safety during the wind shift?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately reposition all crews to pre-identified safety zones and monitor until fires merge.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep crews on the backfire line to maintain control despite the wind shift.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The backfire successfully stopped the main fire. The farmstead is intact and all personnel are safe. Overhaul operations are underway.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
